title: Hospital Discharge Checklist
summary: The other half of a hospital stay — making sure nothing gets lost in the rush of leaving.

tasks.title: Get a complete, written medication list
tasks.explanation: Including what's stopped, changed, or newly added.

tasks.title: Read the list back to confirm it matches what you're hearing
tasks.explanation: Catches errors before they become a problem at home.

tasks.title: Fill any new prescriptions before or immediately after leaving
tasks.explanation: Avoids a gap in a critical medication.

tasks.title: Confirm the first follow-up appointment is already scheduled
Get a direct number for questions in the first 48 hours
tasks.explanation: Not just a general office line.

tasks.title: Ask what specific symptoms warrant a call vs. an ER visit
tasks.explanation: Concrete signs, not a general "call if worried."

tasks.title: Confirm any home equipment is ready
tasks.explanation: Hospital bed, walker, shower chair, oxygen, as applicable.
tasks.who: Equipment supplier or home health team.

tasks.title: Do a quick home safety check for the new level of need
tasks.explanation: Especially if mobility has changed during the stay.
tasks.notes: See Home Safety Checklist for the full version.

tasks.title: Confirm home health or follow-up care referrals are actually scheduled
